trigger.reason: Anthropic released Opus 4.8 with Dynamic Workflows agent-coordination tool — a new model release with capability demo meeting all trigger criteria and likely to spark sympathy-fade selling in low-code, workflow automation, and customer-service software stocks.

primary_event.headline: Anthropic releases Opus 4.8 with new 'dynamic workflow' tool
primary_event.summary: Anthropic has released Opus 4.8, a new model version featuring Dynamic Workflows, a tool designed to coordinate swarms of subagents. This capability targets enterprise workflow automation and agentic task orchestration, directly competing with no-code/low-code platforms and workflow automation software.

secondary_events.headline: Anthropic raises $65 billion, nears $1T valuation ahead of IPO

discovery.trigger_acknowledgment: Analyzed Anthropic's Opus 4.8 release with Dynamic Workflows agent-coordination tool, which targets workflow automation, low-code platforms, and customer-service software.
discovery.run_summary: The trigger is an Anthropic agent-orchestration capability — the blast radius should be workflow/RPA/customer-service software, NOT semiconductors, healthcare analytics, or municipal services. Most candidates here are thematically swept rather than directly threatened. The semis names (PLAB, ONTO, AEIS) are mechanically backwards — AI capex needs more masks, more process control, more power delivery. Several SaaS names rallied on the news, suggesting the market is already partially sorting signal from noise.

discoveries.name: Photronics, Inc.
discoveries.filings_evidence: Risk Factors discuss photomask demand tied to IC/FPD design activity, customer concentration, and capex cycles — no mention of AI/agent software as a competitive threat. Photomasks are a physical input to every chip including AI accelerators.
discoveries.thesis: Photronics has zero business-model overlap with Anthropic's agent-orchestration capability. The magnitude of today's drop is far beyond a sympathy fade and suggests company-specific news (possibly earnings/guidance — 8x volume, -36%). Cannot confidently classify as pure sympathy-fade without confirming no negative catalyst.
discoveries.what_confirms: Confirm there was no earnings miss, guidance cut, or major customer loss announcement today. If the move is purely sector sympathy, the magnitude is extreme and the bounce setup is strong.
discoveries.what_kills: An earnings miss, lost-customer 8-K, or impairment charge today would invalidate the sympathy-fade thesis entirely — this move is too large to be pure trigger sympathy.
discoveries.what_to_learn: Moves of -30%+ on 8x volume rarely reflect pure sympathy — always check for concurrent company-specific catalyst before calling overdone.
discoveries.catalyst: Anthropic releases Opus 4.8 with new 'dynamic workflow' tool
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Semi-cap names often get swept in tech-wide AI-day risk-off, though mechanically AI buildout increases photomask demand.
research_pointers: Check for same-day earnings release or 8-K filing
Verify no customer-concentration disclosure (top-2 customers are 29% of revenue)
Check tariff-related guidance given recent Section 232 semiconductor investigation

discoveries.name: Onto Innovation Inc.
discoveries.filings_evidence: Risk Factors discuss semiconductor cyclicality, customer concentration, and competitive R&D — Anthropic-style agent orchestration has no overlap with process-control metrology for advanced nodes. AI is mentioned as an internal tool risk, not a competitive threat.
discoveries.thesis: Onto sells process-control/metrology equipment essential for advanced-node fabs. The Anthropic agent-orchestration release has no mechanical pathway to reduce demand for semiconductor inspection equipment — if anything, AI accelerator complexity increases inspection intensity. A modest drop on an Anthropic-day is a classic mistaken sympathy fade.
discoveries.what_confirms: AI accelerator volume growth at TSMC/Samsung/Intel; no negative semi-cap guidance from peers; no customer-specific news at major Onto customers.
discoveries.what_kills: Genuine semi-cap cycle inflection (memory or logic capex cut), or a customer-concentration shock at a top buyer.
discoveries.what_to_learn: Semi-cap equipment is mechanically a BENEFICIARY of AI buildout — sympathy fades on AI-lab news days create asymmetric entries.
discoveries.catalyst: Anthropic releases Opus 4.8 with new 'dynamic workflow' tool
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Curated as semis_picks_shovels false-victim; sells process control for advanced nodes that AI chips require.
research_pointers: Confirm no concurrent earnings/guidance from Onto
Cross-check with KLA, AMAT, LRCX same-day action — if they're flat/up, Onto's drop is idiosyncratic
Track HBM and advanced packaging metrology demand commentary

discoveries.name: Advanced Energy Industries, Inc
discoveries.filings_evidence: Risk Factors explicitly highlight AI as a TAILWIND: 'infrastructure investments in artificial intelligence (AI) have increased substantially, which is driving significant demand increases in the Data Center Computing market.' No exposure to agent-orchestration software.
discoveries.thesis: AEIS provides power delivery for fabs and data centers — both of which expand with AI buildout. The 10-K explicitly names AI as a demand driver. A drop on an Anthropic agent-tool release is mechanically backwards: more AI agents running means more inference compute means more data center power conditioning demand.
discoveries.what_confirms: Continued AI-driven data center capex commentary; hyperscaler capex guidance unchanged; semi-cap peer trading flat.
discoveries.what_kills: Data center capex slowdown signal from a hyperscaler; major customer loss; semi-cycle downturn.
discoveries.what_to_learn: Power-delivery infrastructure (AEIS, VRT, ETN) is a direct AI-capex beneficiary — sympathy fades on AI-lab capability news are textbook misreads.
discoveries.catalyst: Anthropic releases Opus 4.8 with new 'dynamic workflow' tool
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Curated as semis/data-center picks-and-shovels false-victim; 10-K directly identifies AI as driving Data Center Computing demand.
research_pointers: Cross-check with Vertiv (VRT), Eaton (ETN) for sector context
Monitor hyperscaler capex guidance from MSFT, META, GOOGL, AMZN

No filings available (10-K/10-Q both null); cannot perform Risk Factors analysis. Note: this is the real_victim_control — voice telephony IS plausibly threatened by voice agents at parity, so absence of filings denies us the test.

discovery.no_signals_note: Most candidates rallied today rather than selling off, reflecting an unusually clean tape where the market correctly distinguished workflow-automation targets from AI-adjacent SaaS with embedded data/integration moats. The only meaningful drops were in semi-cap names (PLAB, ONTO, AEIS) where the AI-panic read is mechanically backwards — though PLAB's -36% is too extreme to be pure sympathy and warrants concurrent-catalyst verification before sizing.
